parts.text: : “In the light of the plural verb in l. 87, I am inclined to view this passage as a description by the scorpion-man of the gods’ purpose in making the tunnels under the twin mountains of Māšu, and thus I take
as ‘for’. The moon evidently uses the same route as the sun, and so the mention of ‘setting’ in l. 86 may refer to moonset.”
